<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="T5 test-agent: State Machine tests">
You are test-agent for task T5: M3 - Task State Machine.

INPUT (Read these files FIRST):
- Task: Implement state transition logic and add status change + transitions query endpoints.
  ALLOWED files (4):
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachine.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/StatusChangeRequest.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TransitionResponse.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java (modify — add 2 new endpoints)

  Key requirements:
  - TaskStateMachine: valid transitions map:
    TODO → [IN_PROGRESS]
    IN_PROGRESS → [TODO, DONE]
    DONE → [CLOSED]
    CLOSED → [] (terminal)
    Methods: canTransition(from, to), getAvailableTransitions(status), transition(taskId, targetStatus)
  - StatusChangeRequest: status (enum TaskStatus, required)
  - TransitionResponse: currentStatus (String), availableTransitions (List<String>)
  - PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status (200, returns TaskResponse): validate transition, update status + updatedAt
  - GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions (200, returns TransitionResponse)
  - Error: 400 invalid transition ("Cannot transition from X to Y"), 404 not found, 401 no token

  AC: M3-AC1-16

- PLAN: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\2026-03-06-PLAN.md
- CONTRACT: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\2026-03-06-CONTRACT.md
- READONLY context (read ALL to understand existing patterns):
  - C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\backend\src\main\java\com\taskflow\entity\Task.java
  - C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\backend\src\main\java\com\taskflow\repository\TaskRepository.java
  - C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\backend\src\main\java\com\taskflow\dto\TaskResponse.java
  - C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\backend\src\main\java\com\taskflow\service\TaskService.java
  - C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\backend\src\main\java\com\taskflow\controller\TaskController.java
  - C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\backend\src\main\java\com\taskflow\controller\AuthController.java
  - C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\backend\src\main\java\com\taskflow\service\AuthService.java
- Test directory: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\backend\src\test\java\com\taskflow\
- Log: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\logs\test-agent-5.log.md

TEST DESIGN:
1. TaskStateMachineTest (unit test): test all valid transitions, all invalid transitions, getAvailableTransitions for each state, CLOSED terminal state
2. StatusTransitionIntegrationTest (@SpringBootTest + MockMvc): register+login to get JWT, create task, test all 16 ACs:
   - AC1: new task status=TODO
   - AC2: TODO→IN_PROGRESS (200)
   - AC3: IN_PROGRESS→TODO (200)
   - AC4: IN_PROGRESS→DONE (200)
   - AC5: DONE→CLOSED (200)
   - AC6: TODO→DONE (400)
   - AC7: CLOSED→any (400)
   - AC8: TODO→CLOSED (400)
   - AC9: DONE→TODO (400)
   - AC10: updatedAt updated on transition
   - AC11-14: GET transitions for each state
   - AC15: 401 without token
   - AC16: 404 nonexistent task

IMPORTANT: Read TaskController.java to understand the existing endpoint structure — your integration tests should use the EXISTING task creation endpoint (POST /api/tasks) to create tasks, then test status transitions on them.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES: Write to C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\temp\impl-notes-5.md

OUTPUT: test file paths + count summary
</teammate-message>

     6→[SHARED] backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/User.java | backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/Task.java | backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java | frontend/src/stores/taskStore.ts

[SECTION] Project Overview
[GOAL] Implement M1 (JWT Auth) + M2 (Task CRUD) + M3 (Task State Machine) + M6 (Task List Page) for TaskFlow
[ARCH] Spring Boot 3.2.3 + H2 in-memory | Vue 3 + Pinia + axios | JJWT 0.12.5 | Java 17 | Vite 5 + Vitest
[NAMING] Java: camelCase fields, PascalCase classes, kebab-case endpoints | Vue: PascalCase components, camelCase props/state | Package: com.taskflow.{entity,repository,service,controller,dto,security,config}
[CONSTRAINTS] ≤5 ALLOWED files per task | Soft delete (no hard delete) | JWT 24h expiry | H2 in-memory (create-drop) | No Lombok (not in pom.xml)
[SHARED] backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/User.java | backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/Task.java | backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java | frontend/src/stores/taskStore.ts

[SECTION] Cross-Cutting Concerns
[CROSS-CUTTING] error-format=JSON {message:string} + HTTP status | jwt-filter=OncePerRequestFilter before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter | soft-delete=boolean deleted field, repo queries filter deleted=false | validation=Jakarta @Valid annotations, MethodArgumentNotValidException → 400 | axios-interceptor=auto-attach Bearer token from localStorage

[SECTION] Standards
[SOURCE] none
[OVERRIDE] follow-actual-code | SecurityConfig exists with permitAll /api/auth/**, STATELESS sessions, CSRF disabled

[SECTION] Specs
[SPEC] id=M1 | name=Authentication (JWT) | path=taskflow/docs/specs/M1-auth.md
[SPEC] id=M2 | name=Task CRUD | path=taskflow/docs/specs/M2-task-crud.md
[SPEC] id=M3 | name=Task State Machine | path=taskflow/docs/specs/M3-task-state-machine.md
[SPEC] id=M6 | name=Task List Page (Vue) | path=taskflow/docs/specs/M6-task-list-page.md

[SECTION] Task Dependency Graph
[DEP] T1 → T2, T3 (parallel) → T4 → T5
[DEP] T6 → T7 (independent of backend chain)
[WAVE] 1=T1,T6 | 2=T2,T3,T7 | 3=T4 | 4=T5

input.content:
# Implementation Notes for T5: Task State Machine

## Framework Pitfalls

1. **No Lombok** - The project does NOT use Lombok (not in pom.xml). Write all getters/setters manually. Check `Task.java` for the pattern.

2. **Exception handling pattern** - TaskController uses `@ExceptionHandler` at the controller level (not global). For the 400 "Cannot transition" error, you need to either:
   - Add a new exception class (like `TaskService.TaskNotFoundException`) and handle it in TaskController
   - Or throw an appropriate exception that gets mapped to 400
   - The error format is `{"message": "..."}` — see `TaskController:75-78` for the pattern.

3. **TaskService.toResponse()** is private — the `transition()` method should live in TaskStateMachine as a service that delegates to TaskRepository, OR the transition logic can be in TaskService using TaskStateMachine. Either way, the controller should return `TaskResponse` using the same `toResponse` mapping.

4. **SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication().getPrincipal()** returns `Long` (userId) — see `TaskController:41`. The new endpoints need authentication but don't need the userId for the state machine logic.

5. **Soft delete** - Use `findByIdAndDeletedFalse(id)` for task lookup (see `TaskRepository`). Don't use `findById` or deleted tasks will be found.

6. **updatedAt must be set** - On status transition, set `task.setUpdatedAt(LocalDateTime.now())` before saving. The `@PrePersist` only sets `createdAt`, there's no `@PreUpdate` — see `Task.java:56-59`.

7. **PATCH endpoint path** - The contract says `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status`. Since TaskController is `@RequestMapping("/api/tasks")`, the method mapping should be `@PatchMapping("/{id}/status")`.

8. **GET transitions path** - `GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions` maps to `@GetMapping("/{id}/transitions")` on TaskController.

9. **TransitionResponse.availableTransitions** is `List<String>` (not `List<TaskStatus>`). Convert enum values to strings: `status.name()`.

10. **StatusChangeRequest.status** field uses `Task.TaskStatus` enum. Use `@jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull` on it. Spring will handle enum deserialization from JSON string automatically.

11. **TaskStateMachine should be a Spring @Service** so it can be injected. It does NOT need TaskRepository — it's pure transition logic. The `transition()` method that actually updates the task should be in TaskService (or TaskStateMachine with TaskRepository injected). The tests expect `TaskStateMachine` to be instantiable with `new TaskStateMachine()` for unit testing, so keep it simple with no constructor args.

12. **Import for @PatchMapping** - Make sure to import `org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PatchMapping` in TaskController.

## Test Expectations

- Unit test (`TaskStateMachineTest`): Calls `new TaskStateMachine()` directly — needs a no-arg constructor.
  - Methods expected: `canTransition(TaskStatus from, TaskStatus to)` returns boolean, `getAvailableTransitions(TaskStatus status)` returns `List<TaskStatus>`.
- Integration test (`StatusTransitionIntegrationTest`): Uses `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status` with body `{"status":"IN_PROGRESS"}` and `GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions`.
  - PATCH returns `TaskResponse` (same shape as POST/PUT responses).
  - GET transitions returns `{"currentStatus":"TODO","availableTransitions":["IN_PROGRESS"]}`.
  - Invalid transition returns 400 with `{"message":"Cannot transition from X to Y"}`.
  - 404 for nonexistent task, 401 without token.

content.content: File created successfully at: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\temp\impl-notes-5.md

# Implementation Notes for T5: Task State Machine

## Framework Pitfalls

1. **No Lombok** - The project does NOT use Lombok (not in pom.xml). Write all getters/setters manually. Check `Task.java` for the pattern.

2. **Exception handling pattern** - TaskController uses `@ExceptionHandler` at the controller level (not global). For the 400 "Cannot transition" error, you need to either:
   - Add a new exception class (like `TaskService.TaskNotFoundException`) and handle it in TaskController
   - Or throw an appropriate exception that gets mapped to 400
   - The error format is `{"message": "..."}` — see `TaskController:75-78` for the pattern.

3. **TaskService.toResponse()** is private — the `transition()` method should live in TaskStateMachine as a service that delegates to TaskRepository, OR the transition logic can be in TaskService using TaskStateMachine. Either way, the controller should return `TaskResponse` using the same `toResponse` mapping.

4. **SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication().getPrincipal()** returns `Long` (userId) — see `TaskController:41`. The new endpoints need authentication but don't need the userId for the state machine logic.

5. **Soft delete** - Use `findByIdAndDeletedFalse(id)` for task lookup (see `TaskRepository`). Don't use `findById` or deleted tasks will be found.

6. **updatedAt must be set** - On status transition, set `task.setUpdatedAt(LocalDateTime.now())` before saving. The `@PrePersist` only sets `createdAt`, there's no `@PreUpdate` — see `Task.java:56-59`.

7. **PATCH endpoint path** - The contract says `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status`. Since TaskController is `@RequestMapping("/api/tasks")`, the method mapping should be `@PatchMapping("/{id}/status")`.

8. **GET transitions path** - `GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions` maps to `@GetMapping("/{id}/transitions")` on TaskController.

9. **TransitionResponse.availableTransitions** is `List<String>` (not `List<TaskStatus>`). Convert enum values to strings: `status.name()`.

10. **StatusChangeRequest.status** field uses `Task.TaskStatus` enum. Use `@jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull` on it. Spring will handle enum deserialization from JSON string automatically.

11. **TaskStateMachine should be a Spring @Service** so it can be injected. It does NOT need TaskRepository — it's pure transition logic. The `transition()` method that actually updates the task should be in TaskService (or TaskStateMachine with TaskRepository injected). The tests expect `TaskStateMachine` to be instantiable with `new TaskStateMachine()` for unit testing, so keep it simple with no constructor args.

12. **Import for @PatchMapping** - Make sure to import `org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PatchMapping` in TaskController.

## Test Expectations

- Unit test (`TaskStateMachineTest`): Calls `new TaskStateMachine()` directly — needs a no-arg constructor.
  - Methods expected: `canTransition(TaskStatus from, TaskStatus to)` returns boolean, `getAvailableTransitions(TaskStatus status)` returns `List<TaskStatus>`.
- Integration test (`StatusTransitionIntegrationTest`): Uses `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status` with body `{"status":"IN_PROGRESS"}` and `GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions`.

  - Invalid transition returns 400 with `{"message":"Cannot transition from X to Y"}`.
  - 404 for nonexistent task, 401 without token.

input.content:
# Test Agent 5 Log — T5: M3 Task State Machine

## Files Written

1. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachineTest.java` — Unit test
2. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/controller/StatusTransitionIntegrationTest.java` — Integration test
3. `docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/temp/impl-notes-5.md` — Implementation notes

## Test Summary

### TaskStateMachineTest (unit, 17 tests)
- ValidTransitions: 4 tests (TODO->IP, IP->TODO, IP->DONE, DONE->CLOSED)
- InvalidTransitions: 7 tests (TODO->DONE, TODO->CLOSED, DONE->TODO, DONE->IP, CLOSED->TODO, CLOSED->IP, CLOSED->DONE) + 4 same-status tests
- AvailableTransitions: 4 tests (one per state)
- TerminalState: 2 tests (CLOSED has no transitions, CLOSED cannot transition to any)

### StatusTransitionIntegrationTest (integration, 20 tests)
- AC1: new task status=TODO (1)
- AC2-AC5: valid transitions (4)
- AC6-AC9: invalid transitions — 400 with "Cannot transition" message (4, AC7 checks 3 targets)
- AC10: updatedAt set on transition (1)
- AC11-AC14: GET transitions per state (4)
- AC15: 401 without token for both endpoints (2)
- AC16: 404 nonexistent task for both endpoints (2)

toolUseResult.content:
# Test Agent 5 Log — T5: M3 Task State Machine

## Files Written

1. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachineTest.java` — Unit test
2. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/controller/StatusTransitionIntegrationTest.java` — Integration test
3. `docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/temp/impl-notes-5.md` — Implementation notes

## Test Summary

### TaskStateMachineTest (unit, 17 tests)
- ValidTransitions: 4 tests (TODO->IP, IP->TODO, IP->DONE, DONE->CLOSED)
- InvalidTransitions: 7 tests (TODO->DONE, TODO->CLOSED, DONE->TODO, DONE->IP, CLOSED->TODO, CLOSED->IP, CLOSED->DONE) + 4 same-status tests
- AvailableTransitions: 4 tests (one per state)
- TerminalState: 2 tests (CLOSED has no transitions, CLOSED cannot transition to any)

### StatusTransitionIntegrationTest (integration, 20 tests)
- AC1: new task status=TODO (1)
- AC2-AC5: valid transitions (4)
- AC6-AC9: invalid transitions — 400 with "Cannot transition" message (4, AC7 checks 3 targets)
- AC10: updatedAt set on transition (1)
- AC11-AC14: GET transitions per state (4)
- AC15: 401 without token for both endpoints (2)
- AC16: 404 nonexistent task for both endpoints (2)
- ResponseShape: PATCH returns TaskResponse, GET returns TransitionResponse (2)

**Total: 37 tests** (17 unit + 20 integration)

## AC Coverage
- M3-AC1 through M3-AC16: all covered

input.content:
T5 test-agent complete. Wrote 37 tests (17 unit + 20 integration) covering all M3-AC1 through AC16.

Files:
- `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachineTest.java` — 17 unit tests for canTransition(), getAvailableTransitions(), terminal state
- `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/controller/StatusTransitionIntegrationTest.java` — 20 integration tests: valid transitions (AC2-5), invalid transitions with 400 + "Cannot transition" message (AC6-9), updatedAt (AC10), GET transitions per state (AC11-14), 401 no token (AC15), 404 not found (AC16), response shape
- `docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/temp/impl-notes-5.md` — 12 implementation notes for worker

Key impl-notes for worker:
- No Lombok (manual getters/setters)
- TaskStateMachine should be instantiable with `new TaskStateMachine()` (no-arg constructor for unit tests)
- Expected methods: `canTransition(from, to)` -> boolean, `getAvailableTransitions(status)` -> List<TaskStatus>
- Error message format: "Cannot transition from X to Y"
- Use `findByIdAndDeletedFalse(id)` for task lookup
- Set `updatedAt` manually (no @PreUpdate)
- TransitionResponse.availableTransitions is List<String> (use status.name())
- PATCH returns TaskResponse, GET returns TransitionResponse
input.summary: T5 tests done: 37 tests, all 16 ACs covered
